body_txt: When giraffes appear in fiction, they're often nice and heroic. There are quite a few reasons for this, although it may mainly be based on their appearance. Their bright golden fur and recognizable spot pattern look appealing, and their Long Necks are often seen as comical. Their tall bodies, long eyelashes, and short, rounded horns give them a Gentle Giant appearance. It helps that real giraffes actually are fairly passive. They aren't very territorial but very social. Giraffes do fight, with their horns and long necks, and may defend themselves from predators with devastatingly powerful kicks, but they're more well known for striding gracefully across the savanna. Also, it's rare for them to attack humans. As a result, fictional giraffes tend to be gentle, caring characters rather than fighters. It also helps that giraffes are ungulates, a group of hoofed mammals that also includes deer , pigs, and other animals that often have good reputations. The okapi does not appear in fiction often enough to have its own trope, but when one does it will typically be as amicable as its cousin, with a massive dose of Shrinking Violet . Sub-Trope of Herbivores Are Friendly . Often overlaps with The Quiet One due to the myth that giraffes are mute. Compare The Marvelous Deer , Honorable Elephant , and Gentle Gorilla for the other tropes regarding gentle giant herbivores. Also compare Huggy, Huggy Hippos , although hippos are much less passive in real life than giraffes.
